description: The introduction of the No Child Left Behind Act (NCLB) in the US mandated standardized testing to hold schools accountable. The debate saw initial broad bipartisan support that eroded as teachers' unions and local districts criticized the 'teaching to the test' pedagogy.

key_events.description: President Bush signs NCLB into law with bipartisan fanfare.
First wave of schools designated as 'failing' creates local backlash.
National Education Association files lawsuit against US Department of Education.
Public polling confirms majority opposition to testing mandates.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring the decay of bipartisan consensus on federal social mandates.
